evidence: The amber probe is the brightest, most saturated element and clearly leads; the periwinkle candidates and lilac score bars recede as supporting context.
The probe, four fixed candidate arrows, and four score bars form one coherent scoring system rather than five unrelated novel ideas introduced at equal weight.
Labels c1..c4 sit radially near their arrow tips and are repeated directly under each score bar, and the probe is labeled on its shaft; there is no boxed legend or color key.
The only running text is a short two-line instruction that names the visible probe and c1 handle; every other string is a label on an object.
The heading is clearly legible and the small low-contrast mono readouts (0/1/-1 and c1..c4) remain readable, if borderline in size, at this resolution.
The probe, all candidate arrows, and all score bars sit fully inside the frame with generous margins at both 1280 and 375 px widths.

The white active-handle selection box and amber probe dot overlap the 'probe' label in the aligned and anti-aligned frames (the label reads 'prob○') and the box overlaps the 'c1' label in the length-cheat frame.
suggestion: Offset the probe and c1 labels farther from their draggable handles so the selection box and handle dot never sit on top of the text.

evidence: The amber probe (attention/active element), periwinkle candidate arrows, lilac score bars, and cream labels follow consistent Night School palette semantics with no concept switching color.
The white boxes mark which handle is currently active/draggable, an affordance with meaning; there are no mascots, ornaments, or filler graphics.
The probe, four fixed candidates, c1's length handle, live lilac score bars with numeric readouts, the best-aligned bar leading, and a negative c1 readout in anti-aligned are all present as promised.
The four score bars sit far below the arrow diagram with a large empty band between them, forcing a long vertical jump from each candidate arrow to its distant score bar.
suggestion: Move the score bars nearer the arrows or draw light connectors so each candidate's direction and its bar read together.

evidence: not applicable: the images are discrete driven states rather than a single build sequence, so no morph path can be judged from stills.
Margins are ample and the reading order runs top-to-bottom, though a large empty vertical band separates the diagram cluster from the bar cluster.
